lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-00000002EDF5.0B2-What reason brings you to travel so far from home_.mp3
lines.transcript: What reason brings you to travel so far from home?

lines.speaker: Hanzo
lines.filename: 2-Hanzo-00000002F2D2.0B2-I was banished from my home for taking my brother's life. I can never return.mp3
lines.transcript: I was banished from my home for taking my brother's life. I can never return.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-00000002EDF3.0B2-Why fight for this lord_ You have never cared much for the nobility.mp3
lines.transcript: Why fight for this lord? You have never cared much for the nobility.

lines.speaker: Soldier_ 76
lines.filename: 2-Soldier_ 76-00000002EF05.0B2-It is true. But today we share a common foe_ the Reaper.mp3
lines.transcript: It is true. But today we share a common foe: the Reaper.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-00000002EDF4.0B2-I once met a man who could summon a dragon as you do.mp3
lines.transcript: I once met a man who could summon a dragon as you do.

lines.speaker: Hanzo
lines.filename: 2-Hanzo-00000002F2D1.0B2-Impossible. Only my clan possesses the skill. My brother could, but he is dead.mp3
lines.transcript: Impossible. Only my clan possesses the skill. My brother could, but he is dead.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-00000002EDEE.0B2-You do not trust my alchemy, do you_.mp3
lines.transcript: You do not trust my alchemy, do you?

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 2-Cassidy-00000002ECE6.0B2-All due respect, witchcraft and magic potions are no match for a good six-shooter.mp3
lines.transcript: All due respect, witchcraft and magic potions are no match for a good six-shooter.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-00000002EDEF.0B2-We must stop these abominations created in the name of science.mp3
lines.transcript: We must stop these abominations created in the name of science.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 2-Cassidy-00000002ECE5.0B2-You have your cause. I'm just looking for a good fight.mp3
lines.transcript: You have your cause. I'm just looking for a good fight.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-00000002B8BF.0B2-Gerard was a fool to love someone like you.mp3
lines.transcript: Gerard was a fool to love someone like you.

lines.speaker: Widowmaker
lines.filename: 2-Widowmaker-00000002A927.0B2-You don't know anything about him.mp3
lines.transcript: You don't know anything about him.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-00000002B8C3.0B2-For a man of your years, you're looking pretty good, Jack.mp3
lines.transcript: For a man of your years, you're looking pretty good, Jack.

lines.speaker: Soldier_ 76
lines.filename: 2-Soldier_ 76-00000002B779.0B2-Well, all that stuff they pumped into me has to be good for something.mp3
lines.transcript: Well, all that stuff they pumped into me has to be good for something.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-00000002B8D1.0B2-What are you going to do when the fighting's over, Jack_.mp3
lines.transcript: What are you going to do when the fighting's over, Jack?

lines.speaker: Soldier_ 76
lines.filename: 2-Soldier_ 76-00000002B76C.0B2-I'm a soldier, Ana. Retirement doesn't suit me.mp3
lines.transcript: I'm a soldier, Ana. Retirement doesn't suit me.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-000000043E60.0B2-Staying out of trouble, Genji_.mp3
lines.transcript: Staying out of trouble, Genji?

lines.speaker: Genji
lines.filename: 2-Genji-000000049E7F.0B2-(laughs) On my best behavior, captain.mp3
lines.transcript: (laughs) On my best behavior, captain.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-00000002B8C2.0B2-Seems like neither of us liked being dead very much.mp3
lines.transcript: Seems like neither of us liked being dead very much.

lines.speaker: Soldier_ 76
lines.filename: 2-Soldier_ 76-00000002B75D.0B2-Old soldiers are hard to kill.mp3
lines.transcript: Old soldiers are hard to kill.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-00000002B8BB.0B2-I'll be watching your back out there, Fareeha.mp3
lines.transcript: I'll be watching your back out there, Fareeha.

lines.speaker: Pharah
lines.filename: 2-Pharah-00000002A8D6.0B2-Then I have nothing to worry about.mp3
lines.transcript: Then I have nothing to worry about.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-00000002B8BD.0B2-Reinhardt, I must say you're looking quite well. This life must agree with you.mp3
lines.transcript: Reinhardt, I must say you're looking quite well. This life must agree with you.

lines.speaker: Reinhardt
lines.filename: 2-Reinhardt-00000002A851.0B2-And you are looking as lovely as ever.mp3
lines.transcript: And you are looking as lovely as ever.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-00000002B8BE.0B2-Just like old times, eh, Reinhardt_.mp3
lines.transcript: Just like old times, eh, Reinhardt?

lines.speaker: Reinhardt
lines.filename: 2-Reinhardt-00000002A852.0B2-We old timers must stick together... teach these kids a thing or two. (laughs).mp3
lines.transcript: We old timers must stick together... teach these kids a thing or two. (laughs)

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-00000002BC94.0B2-That one time_ You mean in Egypt_ Or the time in Russia. Or Brazil_.mp3
lines.transcript: That one time? You mean in Egypt? Or the time in Russia. Or Brazil?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-000000038497.0B2-(sighs) Youth truly is wasted on the young.mp3
lines.transcript: (sighs) Youth truly is wasted on the young.

lines.speaker: D.Va
lines.filename: 2-D.Va-00000002EF98.0B2-Aw, come on, Ana. I bet you got up to all sorts of trouble when you were my age!.mp3
lines.transcript: Aw, come on, Ana. I bet you got up to all sorts of trouble when you were my age!

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-000000052B9C.0B2-(sighs) Youth truly is wasted on the young.mp3
lines.transcript: (sighs) Youth truly is wasted on the young.

lines.speaker: Lúcio
lines.filename: 2-Lúcio-000000036CDB.0B2-Ana, have you seen my schedule_.mp3
lines.transcript: Ana, have you seen my schedule?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-000000052B9D.0B2-(sighs) Youth truly is wasted on the young.mp3
lines.transcript: (sighs) Youth truly is wasted on the young.

lines.speaker: Tracer
lines.filename: 2-Tracer-000000037147.0B2-At least statistically, I've wasted less than most.mp3
lines.transcript: At least statistically, I've wasted less than most.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-00000002B8C0.0B2-It seems I was wrong about you, Amelie.mp3
lines.transcript: It seems I was wrong about you, Amelie.

lines.speaker: Widowmaker
lines.filename: 2-Widowmaker-000000036371.0B2-I thought you'd have gotten used to that feeling by now.mp3
lines.transcript: I thought you'd have gotten used to that feeling by now.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-00000005F12C.0B2-Fighting battles at such a young age... I hope you remember to take time for yourself.mp3
lines.transcript: Fighting battles at such a young age... I hope you remember to take time for yourself.

lines.speaker: D.Va
lines.filename: 2-D.Va-00000005F12E.0B2-Sometimes. But I have so much to do!.mp3
lines.transcript: Sometimes. But I have so much to do!

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 3-Ana-00000005F124.0B2-(sigh) That's always the excuse.mp3
lines.transcript: (sigh) That's always the excuse.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-00000006250E.0B2-I heard Talon failed another mission. Going soft_.mp3
lines.transcript: I heard Talon failed another mission. Going soft?

lines.speaker: Widowmaker
lines.filename: 2-Widowmaker-0000000620ED.0B2-Do not speak to me unless you wish to lose your other eye.mp3
lines.transcript: Do not speak to me unless you wish to lose your other eye.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 3-Ana-00000006250F.0B2-I'd still be the better shot.mp3
lines.transcript: I'd still be the better shot.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-000000062511.0B2-Aren't you a relic_ A Siege Automaton E54... your kind left a grave wound on this world.mp3
lines.transcript: Aren't you a relic? A Siege Automaton E54... your kind left a grave wound on this world.

lines.speaker: Bastion
lines.filename: 2-Bastion-00000006214B.0B2-(hesitant beeps).mp3
lines.transcript: (hesitant beeps)

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 3-Ana-000000062512.0B2-Come to think of it, I suppose we aren't so different.mp3
lines.transcript: Come to think of it, I suppose we aren't so different.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-000000062516.0B2-You're hard to keep a scope on, Genji. Always so unpredictable.mp3
lines.transcript: You're hard to keep a scope on, Genji. Always so unpredictable.

lines.speaker: Genji
lines.filename: 2-Genji-0000000621F7.0B2-Oh. I'm... sorry_.mp3
lines.transcript: Oh. I'm... sorry?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 3-Ana-000000062517.0B2-Don't be. It keeps me sharp.mp3
lines.transcript: Don't be. It keeps me sharp.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-000000062518.0B2-I know that look. Mending an old wound_.mp3
lines.transcript: I know that look. Mending an old wound?

lines.speaker: Hanzo
lines.filename: 2-Hanzo-000000061F87.0B2-I wronged someone dear to me. The path to redemption is… trying.mp3
lines.transcript: I wronged someone dear to me. The path to redemption is&  trying.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 3-Ana-000000062519.0B2-It's not pleasant. But you'll see it was worth it in the end.mp3
lines.transcript: It's not pleasant. But you'll see it was worth it in the end.

lines.speaker: Hanzo
lines.filename: 4-Hanzo-000000061F88.0B2-I hope so.mp3
lines.transcript: I hope so.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-000000062EFA.0B2-The older I get, the faster the years go.mp3
lines.transcript: The older I get, the faster the years go.

lines.speaker: Sigma
lines.filename: 2-Sigma-0000000634F8.0B2-It’s likely that time is passing at the same rate it always has. (chuckle) Barring relativistic effects,.mp3
lines.transcript: It s likely that time is passing at the same rate it always has. (chuckle) Barring relativistic effects, of course.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 3-Ana-000000062EFB.0B2-I liked you better when you were talking to yourself.mp3
lines.transcript: I liked you better when you were talking to yourself.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-000000062EFD.0B2-You need to stop, Jack. This crusade is getting out of hand.mp3
lines.transcript: You need to stop, Jack. This crusade is getting out of hand.

lines.speaker: Soldier_ 76
lines.filename: 2-Soldier_ 76-000000062F67.0B2-How many times have you told me that_.mp3
lines.transcript: How many times have you told me that?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 3-Ana-000000062EFE.0B2-How many times have I been wrong_.mp3
lines.transcript: How many times have I been wrong?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-0000000630ED.0B2-I never knew you to be a show-off, Genji.mp3
lines.transcript: I never knew you to be a show-off, Genji.

lines.speaker: Genji
lines.filename: 2-Genji-0000000630EE.0B2-Only a fool wastes an opportunity.mp3
lines.transcript: Only a fool wastes an opportunity.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-0000000640FF.0B2-Not a conventional combination.mp3
lines.transcript: Not a conventional combination...

lines.speaker: Zenyatta
lines.filename: 2-Zenyatta-000000063AAD.0B2-... but intimidating, nonetheless.mp3
lines.transcript: ... but intimidating, nonetheless.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-000000064102.0B2-He does good work, doesn't he_.mp3
lines.transcript: He does good work, doesn't he?

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 2-Ashe-000000063D1C.0B2-That he does.mp3
lines.transcript: That he does.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-0000000671AE.0B2-Is Fareeha getting enough rest between missions_.mp3
lines.transcript: Is Fareeha getting enough rest between missions?

lines.speaker: Mercy
lines.filename: 2-Mercy-000000065F24.0B2-Ana, your daughter is more than capable of caring for herself.mp3
lines.transcript: Ana, your daughter is more than capable of caring for herself.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 3-Ana-0000000671B1.0B2-She never napped when she was little. She was cranky all the time.mp3
lines.transcript: She never napped when she was little. She was cranky all the time.

lines.speaker: Pharah
lines.filename: 4-Pharah-00000006576E.0B2-Mom! Everyone can hear you!.mp3
lines.transcript: Mom! Everyone can hear you!

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 1-Ana-0000000684FF.0B2-I happened upon some artifacts in an old necropolis. Would you care to look after them_.mp3
lines.transcript: I happened upon some artifacts in an old necropolis. Would you care to look after them?

lines.speaker: Venture
lines.filename: 2-Venture-000000067EF8.0B2-Would I!.mp3
lines.transcript: Would I!

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 3-Ana-0000000684F5.0B2-I should warn you, they might be cursed.mp3
lines.transcript: I should warn you, they might be cursed...

lines.speaker: Venture
lines.filename: 4-Venture-000000067EFA.0B2-Ah! Even better!.mp3
lines.transcript: Ah! Even better!

lines.speaker: Anran
lines.filename: 1-Anran-00000006AB1B.0B2-I wasn't expecting to be thrown into the thick of it so soon after joining Overwatch.mp3
lines.transcript: I wasn't expecting to be thrown into the thick of it so soon after joining Overwatch.

lines.speaker: Wuyang
lines.filename: 2-Wuyang-00000006B1BC.0B2-Yeah... I was hoping we'd have a little more down time.mp3
lines.transcript: Yeah... I was hoping we'd have a little more down time.

lines.speaker: Anran
lines.filename: 3-Anran-00000006AB1C.0B2-Well, we'll get through it. Taking breaks is overrated anyway.mp3
lines.transcript: Well, we'll get through it. Taking breaks is overrated anyway.

lines.speaker: Wuyang
lines.filename: 4-Wuyang-00000006B1BA.0B2-(sigh) To you, maybe.mp3
lines.transcript: (sigh) To you, maybe.

lines.speaker: Anran
lines.filename: 1-Anran-00000006AD9D.0B2-(aggressive sneeze) Whew, I think my allergies are acting up.mp3
lines.transcript: (aggressive sneeze) Whew, I think my allergies are acting up.

lines.speaker: Anran
lines.filename: 3-Anran-00000006AD9E.0B2-Ugh... no wonder.mp3
lines.transcript: Ugh... no wonder.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-00000005575A.0B2-You still have any contacts with Los Muertos_.mp3
lines.transcript: You still have any contacts with Los Muertos?

lines.speaker: Sombra
lines.filename: 2-Sombra-00000005BBF4.0B2-I might. What's in it for me_.mp3
lines.transcript: I might. What's in it for me?

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 3-Ashe-000000055759.0B2-I'd offer you money, but I don't think that's what you're after.mp3
lines.transcript: I'd offer you money, but I don't think that's what you're after.

lines.speaker: Sombra
lines.filename: 4-Sombra-00000005BBF5.0B2-No. So... any good stories about the cowboy_.mp3
lines.transcript: No. So... any good stories about the cowboy?

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-00000005B9BE.0B2-We have a lot in common, renegade. Both of us are on the run.mp3
lines.transcript: We have a lot in common, renegade. Both of us are on the run.

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 2-Baptiste-00000005B8F5.0B2-The difference is_ I'm running AWAY from trouble.mp3
lines.transcript: The difference is: I'm running AWAY from trouble.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 3-Ashe-00000005B9C0.0B2-Where's the fun in that_.mp3
lines.transcript: Where's the fun in that?

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-00000005B9C7.0B2-I've been looking for a medic and a marksman to join my operation. Didn't expect to find both in one.mp3
lines.transcript: I've been looking for a medic and a marksman to join my operation. Didn't expect to find both in one.

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 2-Baptiste-00000005B901.0B2-I'm aware I have a sought after skillset. It's been a  problem, actually.mp3
lines.transcript: I'm aware I have a sought after skillset. It's been a  problem, actually.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-00000005B9BF.0B2-Countess, I imagine you've accumulated quite a few treasures over the years. Run into any thieves_.mp3
lines.transcript: Countess, I imagine you've accumulated quite a few treasures over the years. Run into any thieves?

lines.speaker: Widowmaker
lines.filename: 2-Widowmaker-00000005B810.0B2-My hounds have.mp3
lines.transcript: My hounds have.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-00000005B9C8.0B2-Hey Countess! What do you think about when you're looking down that scope_.mp3
lines.transcript: Hey Countess! What do you think about when you're looking down that scope?

lines.speaker: Widowmaker
lines.filename: 2-Widowmaker-00000005B81A.0B2-The target. Nothing else.mp3
lines.transcript: The target. Nothing else.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-00000005B9CD.0B2-That’s a lot of armor. Though I guess you are a pretty big target.mp3
lines.transcript: That s a lot of armor. Though I guess you are a pretty big target.

lines.speaker: Reinhardt
lines.filename: 2-Reinhardt-00000005B9A6.0B2-Aha! I guess I am!.mp3
lines.transcript: Aha! I guess I am!

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-00000005B9D2.0B2-That rifle is a fine piece of work. Looks a little unwieldy, though.mp3
lines.transcript: That rifle is a fine piece of work. Looks a little unwieldy, though.

lines.speaker: Widowmaker
lines.filename: 2-Widowmaker-00000005B826.0B2-Perhaps in the hands of an amateur.mp3
lines.transcript: Perhaps in the hands of an amateur.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-00000005E510.0B2-They say you're a good shot. Hope you don't mind that I'm better.mp3
lines.transcript: They say you're a good shot. Hope you don't mind that I'm better.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 2-Ana-00000005E530.0B2-I know more than anyone that a head that big is an easy target.mp3
lines.transcript: I know more than anyone that a head that big is an easy target.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-00000005F138.0B2-Can't imagine what kind of bounty they'd put on your head.mp3
lines.transcript: Can't imagine what kind of bounty they'd put on your head.

lines.speaker: Ramattra
lines.filename: 2-Ramattra-00000005F176.0B2-I'm afraid I don't pay much attention to humanity's laws.mp3
lines.transcript: I'm afraid I don't pay much attention to humanity's laws.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 3-Ashe-00000005F141.0B2-Look at that. We've got something in common.mp3
lines.transcript: Look at that. We've got something in common.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-000000062690.0B2-I can't believe we lost such valuable cargo. You would've fetched a fine sum.mp3
lines.transcript: I can't believe we lost such valuable cargo. You would've fetched a fine sum.

lines.speaker: Echo
lines.filename: 2-Echo-0000000614EB.0B2-My condolences for your loss.mp3
lines.transcript: My condolences for your loss.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 3-Ashe-000000062691.0B2-Considering that you have to deal with Cole, I'd say it's really your loss.mp3
lines.transcript: Considering that you have to deal with Cole, I'd say it's really your loss.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-000000062698.0B2-You need a dose of reality, little miss sunshine.mp3
lines.transcript: You need a dose of reality, little miss sunshine.

lines.speaker: Tracer
lines.filename: 2-Tracer-00000006191C.0B2-It doesn't hurt to look on the bright side!.mp3
lines.transcript: It doesn't hurt to look on the bright side!

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 3-Ashe-000000062699.0B2-'Til the sun gets in your eyes.mp3
lines.transcript: 'Til the sun gets in your eyes.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-00000006269A.0B2-A wanted man on the run. I've known a few of you.mp3
lines.transcript: A wanted man on the run. I've known a few of you.

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 2-Baptiste-000000061A77.0B2-I'm only guilty of stealing hearts.mp3
lines.transcript: I'm only guilty of stealing hearts.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 3-Ashe-00000006269B.0B2-Guess it's a good thing I'm heartless.mp3
lines.transcript: Guess it's a good thing I'm heartless.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-000000062D42.0B2-Time to settle who's the best shot.mp3
lines.transcript: Time to settle who's the best shot.

lines.speaker: Widowmaker
lines.filename: 2-Widowmaker-000000062C59.0B2-There is no competition. I was trained by the best.mp3
lines.transcript: There is no competition. I was trained by the best.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 3-Ashe-000000062D43.0B2-And I was trained by the West.mp3
lines.transcript: And I was trained by the West.

lines.speaker: Widowmaker
lines.filename: 4-Widowmaker-000000063FE2.0B2-(sighs) Americans.mp3
lines.transcript: (sighs) Americans.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-000000063D27.0B2-Can you build me a bike_ Money's no object.mp3
lines.transcript: Can you build me a bike? Money's no object.

lines.speaker: Wrecking Ball
lines.filename: 2-Wrecking Ball-00000006395F.0B2-(hamster noises) He is an artist, not a mechanic.mp3
lines.transcript: (hamster noises) He is an artist, not a mechanic.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 3-Ashe-000000063D28.0B2-All right. How about a new chassis for Bob_.mp3
lines.transcript: All right. How about a new chassis for Bob?

lines.speaker: Wrecking Ball
lines.filename: 4-Wrecking Ball-000000063960.0B2-(hamster noises) That is... intriguing.mp3
lines.transcript: (hamster noises) That is... intriguing.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-000000063D1D.0B2-Why the hell'd you give Bob all that firepower_.mp3
lines.transcript: Why the hell'd you give Bob all that firepower?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 2-Ana-000000064105.0B2-He doesn't waste it.mp3
lines.transcript: He doesn't waste it.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-000000064DF2.0B2-I better not see your face in my neck of the woods.mp3
lines.transcript: I better not see your face in my neck of the woods.

lines.speaker: Ramattra
lines.filename: 2-Ramattra-000000064000.0B2-Are you afraid I'll free your servant_.mp3
lines.transcript: Are you afraid I'll free your servant?

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 3-Ashe-000000064DF4.0B2-Lay one finger on Bob and I'll show you what fear means.mp3
lines.transcript: Lay one finger on Bob and I'll show you what fear means.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-000000065427.0B2-Your company likes making headlines.mp3
lines.transcript: Your company likes making headlines.

lines.speaker: Symmetra
lines.filename: 2-Symmetra-0000000647D2.0B2-Yes. A natural byproduct of repairing a chaotic world.mp3
lines.transcript: Yes. A natural byproduct of repairing a chaotic world.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 3-Ashe-000000065428.0B2-Hmpfh, seems more like a tactic to line their pockets, but what do I know_.mp3
lines.transcript: Hmpfh, seems more like a tactic to line their pockets, but what do I know?

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-000000065429.0B2-(Whistling) They make 'em all as big as you where you come from_.mp3
lines.transcript: (Whistling) They make 'em all as big as you where you come from?

lines.speaker: Junker Queen
lines.filename: 2-Junker Queen-000000063BEA.0B2-Nah. I was a special order.mp3
lines.transcript: Nah. I was a special order.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-000000064401.0B2-Castle should be up this way.mp3
lines.transcript: Castle should be up this way.

lines.speaker: Sojourn
lines.filename: 2-Sojourn-000000064402.0B2-Does anyone else feel like we're being watched_.mp3
lines.transcript: Does anyone else feel like we're being watched?

lines.speaker: Junker Queen
lines.filename: 3-Junker Queen-000000064403.0B2-Yeah, and I don't take kindly to people lookin' at me!.mp3
lines.transcript: Yeah, and I don't take kindly to people lookin' at me!

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-0000000646B5.0B2-Door's locked. Won't get to the castle without a key.mp3
lines.transcript: Door's locked. Won't get to the castle without a key.

lines.speaker: Junker Queen
lines.filename: 2-Junker Queen-0000000646B6.0B2-It's locked! Need a key to get to the castle.mp3

lines.transcript: Locked. Not getting to the castle without a key.

lines.speaker: Sojourn
lines.filename: 2-Sojourn-0000000646B8.0B2-Is this town always in trouble when you're here_.mp3

lines.transcript: Unless that's an accusation, stop wasting my time.

lines.speaker: Sojourn

lines.filename: 3-Ashe-0000000646BE.0B2-There ain't a prison around that could keep me locked up. Now get pullin'.mp3

lines.filename: 2-Junker Queen-000000064716.0B2-'Course! He paid through the nose for my carcasses. Dunno why.mp3

lines.filename: 3-Ashe-000000064717.0B2-When a mad scientist asks to buy your corpses, you say _no_.mp3
lines.transcript: When a mad scientist asks to buy your corpses, you say "no".

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-0000000648BC.0B2-Don't reckon you can see if we survive the night_.mp3
lines.transcript: Don't reckon you can see if we survive the night?

lines.speaker: Ashe

lines.transcript: Hmph. 'Course not.

lines.transcript: Bride's gone. She's a tricky one.

lines.speaker: Sojourn
lines.filename: 2-Sojourn-000000064A53.0B2-We can't let her flip any more switches.mp3

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 3-Ashe-000000064A74.0B2-This experiment of hers is downright... unnatural.mp3
lines.transcript: This experiment of hers is downright... unnatural.

lines.speaker: Sojourn
lines.filename: 4-Sojourn-000000064A6F.0B2-I wouldn't go pointing fingers if I were you.mp3
lines.transcript: I wouldn't go pointing fingers if I were you.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-000000064A5C.0B2-Her butler's back for revenge!.mp3
lines.transcript: Her butler's back for revenge!

lines.speaker: Junker Queen
lines.filename: 2-Junker Queen-000000064A67.0B2-Why's everyone want revenge on us_ We didn't do anything.mp3
lines.transcript: Why's everyone want revenge on us? We didn't do anything.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 3-Ashe-000000064A62.0B2-Speak for yourself.mp3
lines.transcript: Speak for yourself.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-000000064DEC.0B2-I hear the fishing's great where you're from.mp3
lines.transcript: I hear the fishing's great where you're from.

lines.speaker: Mauga

lines.transcript: Our fish might be a little big for you.

lines.speaker: Ashe

lines.transcript: (chuckle) There's nothing I can't catch with determination and a little dynamite.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-000000064DEF.0B2-I hear if it weren't for your parents, you'd be as much an outlaw as me.mp3
lines.transcript: I hear if it weren't for your parents, you'd be as much an outlaw as me.

lines.speaker: Lifeweaver

lines.transcript: They're always trying to help, but frankly, I'd prefer they gave up.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 3-Ashe-000000064DF0.0B2-Yeah, well, be careful what you wish for.mp3

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-000000066605.0B2-Need me to smuggle out a trophy for you_.mp3
lines.transcript: Need me to smuggle out a trophy for you?

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 2-Baptiste-000000066606.0B2-Oh please! Your rates are ridiculous.mp3
lines.transcript: Oh please! Your rates are ridiculous.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-000000066603.0B2-Don't think this counts as me joining your cause, Bonebreaker.mp3

lines.speaker: Doomfist

lines.speaker: Ashe

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-00000006660A.0B2-Wonder how much I could smuggle that rock of yours.mp3
lines.transcript: Wonder how much I could smuggle that rock of yours.

lines.filename: 2-Wrecking Ball-000000066609.0B2-(hamster noises) His cargo hold is already full of vital snacks.mp3

lines.transcript: How you making a living without the family fortune?

lines.filename: 2-Lifeweaver-0000000695F8.0B2-Nothing so glamorous as running a gang of outlaws, I'm afraid.mp3
lines.transcript: Nothing so glamorous as running a gang of outlaws, I'm afraid.

lines.filename: 3-Ashe-0000000688D1.0B2-Well, Deadlock Gorge would look mighty fine with a rose garden.mp3

lines.filename: 4-Lifeweaver-0000000695F7.0B2-Tempting. But I really should save the world first.mp3

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 1-Ashe-0000000688C8.0B2-You headed home after this, Haz_.mp3

lines.speaker: Hazard
lines.filename: 2-Hazard-0000000687EC.0B2-Aye. Thought I'd bring Susie by the memorial.mp3

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 3-Ashe-0000000688CB.0B2-You're more sentimental than I thought.mp3

lines.filename: 4-Hazard-0000000687ED.0B2-Aren't all us criminals softer than we say so_.mp3
lines.transcript: Aren't all us criminals softer than we say so?

lines.speaker: Athena
lines.filename: 1-Athena-00000005C625.0B2-Winston, why don't you have any pictures of us together_.mp3
lines.transcript: Winston, why don't you have any pictures of us together?

lines.speaker: Winston
lines.filename: 2-Winston-00000005C610.0B2-You don't have a body, Athena. Not yet.mp3
lines.transcript: You don't have a body, Athena. Not yet.

lines.speaker: Athena
lines.filename: 3-Athena-00000005C62D.0B2-Hmm. I would like to be tall.mp3
lines.transcript: Hmm. I would like to be tall.

lines.speaker: Winston
lines.filename: 4-Winston-00000005C60E.0B2-Noted.mp3
lines.transcript: Noted.

lines.speaker: Athena
lines.filename: 1-Athena-000000060EAD.0B2-Winston, have you considered almond butter_.mp3

lines.speaker: Winston
lines.filename: 2-Winston-000000060EAE.0B2-Almond butter_.mp3
lines.transcript: Almond butter?

lines.speaker: Athena
lines.filename: 3-Athena-000000060EAF.0B2-It is widely considered to be a healthier alternative to peanut butter.mp3
lines.transcript: It is widely considered to be a healthier alternative to peanut butter.

lines.speaker: Winston
lines.filename: 4-Winston-000000060EB0.0B2-Sounds like an alternative to good taste.mp3
lines.transcript: Sounds like an alternative to good taste...

lines.speaker: Athena
lines.filename: 1-Athena-000000060ECC.0B2-You should mind your caffeine intake, Winston.mp3
lines.transcript: You should mind your caffeine intake, Winston.

lines.speaker: Winston
lines.filename: 2-Winston-000000060ECB.0B2-I'm too big for a little coffee to get to me!.mp3
lines.transcript: I'm too big for a little coffee to get to me!

lines.speaker: Athena
lines.filename: 3-Athena-000000060EC4.0B2-My records indicate lower sleep quality directly correlated to your caffeine consumption.mp3
lines.transcript: My records indicate lower sleep quality directly correlated to your caffeine consumption.

lines.speaker: Winston
lines.filename: 4-Winston-000000060ECA.0B2-…I guess I could switch to half-caf.mp3

lines.speaker: Winston
lines.filename: 2-Winston-000000060EB6.0B2-One of my favorites. And he even signed it!.mp3

lines.speaker: Athena
lines.filename: 3-Athena-000000060EC0.0B2-Will it still play properly if it is signed_.mp3

lines.speaker: Winston
lines.filename: 4-Winston-000000060EB7.0B2-That... is an excellent question.mp3

lines.filename: 1-Athena-000000060EBF.0B2-Why do humans still listen to records_.mp3
lines.transcript: Why do humans still listen to records?

lines.speaker: Athena
lines.filename: 3-Athena-000000060EBE.0B2-Truly_.mp3

lines.speaker: Winston
lines.filename: 4-Winston-000000060EB9.0B2-Well… That's what Lucio told me.mp3
lines.transcript: Well&  That's what Lucio told me.

lines.speaker: Athena
lines.filename: 1-Athena-000000066D32.0B2-Should I reopen Torbjorn's file_.mp3

lines.filename: 2-Winston-000000066D34.0B2-Wouldn't that be presumptuous_.mp3

lines.speaker: Winston
lines.filename: 3-Winston-000000066D39.0B2-That might be preemptive.mp3

lines.filename: 4-Athena-000000066D36.0B2-It would be responsible to review his tenure as an agent in the event that he returns.mp3

lines.transcript: The receipt says "Damage Per-Simmon".

lines.speaker: Athena

lines.speaker: Winston
lines.filename: 10-Winston-000000066DD1.0B2-I look forward to trying it.mp3

lines.filename: 1-Athena-000000066F42.0B2-Welcome back, Winston. I have created files for our newest agents in the Intel Database.mp3

lines.filename: 2-Winston-000000066FE3.0B2-Excellent! It seems like everyone is settling in.mp3

lines.filename: 1-Baptiste-00000005846A.0B2-Sombra… how is he_.mp3

lines.filename: 2-Sombra-00000005BBF6.0B2-Eager to see you again. Be careful.mp3

lines.speaker: Widowmaker

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 1-Baptiste-000000058419.0B2-Healing and hurting… I guess we're not that different.mp3

lines.speaker: Moira
lines.filename: 2-Moira-000000060433.0B2-I assure you, we are nothing alike.mp3

lines.filename: 1-Baptiste-00000005841C.0B2-I'll be backing you up, so try not to do something too stupid.mp3
lines.transcript: I'll be backing you up, so try not to do something too stupid.

lines.filename: 1-Baptiste-000000058418.0B2-It's good to be working with you again, Dr. Ziegler.mp3

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 1-Baptiste-000000058468.0B2-Reaper! Don't sneak up on me like that.mp3

lines.transcript: Just a reminder that you'll never see me coming.

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 1-Baptiste-00000005B8FE.0B2-I saw a huge lizard with wings not far from here. Anybody know what that's called_.mp3
lines.transcript: I saw a huge lizard with wings not far from here. Anybody know what that's called?

lines.speaker: Torbjörn
lines.filename: 2-Torbjörn-00000005B84E.0B2-A dragon! Where_.mp3
lines.transcript: A dragon! Where?

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 3-Baptiste-00000005B8FF.0B2-(laughs) Oh, the look on your face. I'm dying.mp3
lines.transcript: (laughs) Oh, the look on your face. I'm dying.

lines.filename: 4-Torbjörn-00000005B84F.0B2-I don't get it. The dragon, though. Where exactly did you see it_.mp3
lines.transcript: I don't get it. The dragon, though. Where exactly did you see it?

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 1-Baptiste-00000005B8F9.0B2-Any tips for life on the run, Outlaw_.mp3
lines.transcript: Any tips for life on the run, Outlaw?

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 2-Ashe-00000005B9C5.0B2-Don’t trust anyone. Oh, and uh, never miss an opportunity to wash your socks.mp3
lines.transcript: Don t trust anyone. Oh, and uh, never miss an opportunity to wash your socks.

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 1-Baptiste-000000061A67.0B2-Have you all seen what this guy can do_.mp3

lines.transcript: (bashful beeps)

lines.speaker: Pharah
lines.filename: 2-Pharah-0000000620AE.0B2-Just hit me in midair. How hard could it be_.mp3
lines.transcript: Just hit me in midair. How hard could it be?

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 1-Baptiste-000000061A6C.0B2-Knock knock!.mp3
lines.transcript: Knock knock!

lines.transcript: Who's there?

lines.speaker: Mei
lines.filename: 3-Mei-0000000623C1.0B2-Who's there_.mp3
lines.transcript: Who's there?

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 4-Baptiste-000000061A6F.0B2-Atch!.mp3
lines.transcript: Atch!

lines.speaker: Pharah
lines.filename: 5-Pharah-0000000620B0.0B2-Atch who_.mp3
lines.transcript: Atch who?

lines.speaker: Mei
lines.filename: 6-Mei-0000000623C2.0B2-Atch who_.mp3
lines.transcript: Atch who?

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 7-Baptiste-000000061A72.0B2-Sounds like you have a cold. Let me heal you up!.mp3
lines.transcript: Sounds like you have a cold. Let me heal you up!

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 1-Baptiste-00000005E50F.0B2-I've heard stories about you, Captain Amari... but none of them mentioned how beautiful you are.mp3
lines.transcript: I've heard stories about you, Captain Amari... but none of them mentioned how beautiful you are.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 2-Ana-00000005E50E.0B2-How cute. Your aim had better outdo your flattery, young man.mp3
lines.transcript: How cute. Your aim had better outdo your flattery, young man.

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 1-Baptiste-00000005F16F.0B2-You sure you want me keeping you alive_ I am a human, after all.mp3
lines.transcript: You sure you want me keeping you alive? I am a human, after all.

lines.speaker: Ramattra
lines.filename: 2-Ramattra-00000005F13F.0B2-A helpful human would certainly be a welcome change of pace.mp3
lines.transcript: A helpful human would certainly be a welcome change of pace.

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 1-Baptiste-000000061A69.0B2-I'm surprised you're sticking around.mp3

lines.speaker: Cassidy

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 3-Baptiste-000000061A6B.0B2-True, nobody does _second most handsome man on the team_ better than you.mp3

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 1-Baptiste-00000005FC28.0B2-I've been lifting a lot lately, Zarya. Think I might be catching up to you.mp3

lines.speaker: Baptiste

lines.filename: 1-Baptiste-00000006271D.0B2-Hey Cassidy. Want to know the bounty Talon had on you_.mp3

lines.transcript: Dammit.

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 1-Baptiste-000000061A7F.0B2-Always looking sharp, brother.mp3
lines.transcript: Always looking sharp, brother.

lines.speaker: Hanzo
lines.filename: 2-Hanzo-000000061F8F.0B2-My arrows will never dull, as long as my will stands strong.mp3

lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-00000005503E.0B2-Is it magic that you do_.mp3

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-00000005503D.0B2-Why did you come here_.mp3
lines.transcript: Why did you come here?

lines.speaker: Zenyatta
lines.filename: 2-Zenyatta-000000055593.0B2-I am following in my master's footsteps, searching for enlightenment, though my path has not always be.mp3
lines.transcript: I am following in my master's footsteps, searching for enlightenment, though my path has not always been a straight one.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-000000055046.0B2-I'm really looking forward to fighting with you, Pharah.mp3
lines.transcript: I'm really looking forward to fighting with you, Pharah.

lines.speaker: Pharah
lines.filename: 2-Pharah-00000004CE22.0B2-We Overwatch brats have to stick together.mp3
lines.transcript: We Overwatch brats have to stick together.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-00000005507C.0B2-What brings you here, gunslinger_.mp3
lines.transcript: What brings you here, gunslinger?

lines.speaker: Cassidy

lines.transcript: People hire me to hunt monsters... werewolves, vampires, witches, talkative scientists...

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-000000055076.0B2-Hey, D.Va... who's your mechanic_ They do good work.mp3
lines.transcript: Hey, D.Va... who's your mechanic? They do good work.

lines.filename: 2-D.Va-000000057C8F.0B2-Dae-hyun! He's great, isn't he_.mp3
lines.transcript: Dae-hyun! He's great, isn't he?

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-00000005ABCE.0B2-Let's show them some Swedish hospitality!.mp3
lines.transcript: Let's show them some Swedish hospitality!

lines.speaker: Reinhardt
lines.filename: 2-Reinhardt-000000059806.0B2-For honor and glory!.mp3
lines.transcript: For honor and glory!

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-00000005C05E.0B2-Hey, did you read Märta's latest, Papa_ _Dawn of the Empire of Dawnfire_.mp3
lines.transcript: Hey, did you read Märta's latest, Papa? "Dawn of the Empire of Dawnfire?"

lines.speaker: Torbjörn
lines.filename: 2-Torbjörn-00000005C05F.0B2-Not yet, but I'll get to it.mp3
lines.transcript: Not yet, but I'll get to it.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 3-Brigitte-00000005C060.0B2-I actually think she's gotten a lot better.mp3
lines.transcript: I actually think she's gotten a lot better.

lines.speaker: Reinhardt
lines.filename: 4-Reinhardt-0000000616B4.0B2-She wrote another one!_.mp3
lines.transcript: She wrote another one!?

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-000000061284.0B2-If these Breachers weren't so dangerous, I'd think they were kind of cute.mp3
lines.transcript: If these Breachers weren't so dangerous, I'd think they were kind of cute.

lines.transcript: (timid beeps)

lines.speaker: Reinhardt
lines.filename: 3-Reinhardt-000000061283.0B2-Bah! That's exactly what they want you to think!.mp3
lines.transcript: Bah! That's exactly what they want you to think!

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-00000005EBF9.0B2-Keep those defenses up!.mp3
lines.transcript: Keep those defenses up!

lines.speaker: Torbjörn
lines.filename: 2-Torbjörn-00000005EBFA.0B2-Brigitte has grown into such a leader.mp3
lines.transcript: Brigitte has grown into such a leader.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 3-Brigitte-00000005EBFB.0B2-Papa!.mp3
lines.transcript: Papa!

lines.speaker: Torbjörn
lines.filename: 4-Torbjörn-00000005EBFC.0B2-If you're starting Overwatch again, I really think she should be in charge.mp3
lines.transcript: If you're starting Overwatch again, I really think she should be in charge.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 5-Brigitte-00000005EBFD.0B2-Papa, you're embarrassing me!.mp3
lines.transcript: Papa, you're embarrassing me!

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-00000005EF4E.0B2-Trust me, everyone... with Bastion on our side, this is going to go great!.mp3
lines.transcript: Trust me, everyone... with Bastion on our side, this is going to go great!

lines.speaker: Bastion
lines.filename: 2-Bastion-00000005EF5D.0B2-(sheepish beeps).mp3
lines.transcript: (sheepish beeps)

lines.speaker: Junkrat
lines.filename: 3-Junkrat-00000005EF4F.0B2-Who, the bot_ I thought he was decorative!.mp3
lines.transcript: Who, the bot? I thought he was decorative!

lines.speaker: Pharah
lines.filename: 4-Pharah-00000005EF5C.0B2-Just make sure you're shooting them and not me, please.mp3
lines.transcript: Just make sure you're shooting them and not me, please.

lines.speaker: Reaper
lines.filename: 5-Reaper-00000005EF50.0B2-Looks like a pile of outdated junk to me.mp3
lines.transcript: Looks like a pile of outdated junk to me.

lines.filename: 6-Hanzo-00000005EF51.0B2-Hmpfh.mp3

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 8-Brigitte-00000005EF5B.0B2-Well I'm happy to have you.mp3
lines.transcript: Well I'm happy to have you.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-0000000625E2.0B2-Isn't it reckless to pull enemies toward us_.mp3
lines.transcript: Isn't it reckless to pull enemies toward us?

lines.speaker: Roadhog
lines.filename: 2-Roadhog-000000062203.0B2-(laughs) Afraid of getting too close_.mp3
lines.transcript: (laughs) Afraid of getting too close?

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 3-Brigitte-0000000625E7.0B2-Nope, just thinking of the team. It would help if you did too.mp3
lines.transcript: Nope, just thinking of the team. It would help if you did too.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-0000000625E3.0B2-Can I look at the wings on your suit again_.mp3
lines.transcript: Can I look at the wings on your suit again?

lines.speaker: Pharah
lines.filename: 2-Pharah-0000000620B8.0B2-I thought you preferred to stay on the ground.mp3
lines.transcript: I thought you preferred to stay on the ground.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 3-Brigitte-0000000625E4.0B2-It's for a different idea I had. I just thought the view from up there would be... inspirational.mp3
lines.transcript: It's for a different idea I had. I just thought the view from up there would be... inspirational.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-0000000625E5.0B2-Lucio, have you ever written lyrics before_.mp3
lines.transcript: Lucio, have you ever written lyrics before?

lines.speaker: Lúcio
lines.filename: 2-Lúcio-000000061DC6.0B2-I've dabbled a bit. What's up_.mp3
lines.transcript: I've dabbled a bit. What's up?

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 3-Brigitte-0000000625E6.0B2-I was wondering if you would look at... Actually, I'm going to work on it a bit more.mp3
lines.transcript: I was wondering if you would look at... Actually, I'm going to work on it a bit more.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-0000000625E9.0B2-Reinhardt, do you think I should get a helmet_.mp3
lines.transcript: Reinhardt, do you think I should get a helmet?

lines.speaker: Reinhardt
lines.filename: 2-Reinhardt-0000000616B6.0B2-Do you intend to be struck in the head_.mp3
lines.transcript: Do you intend to be struck in the head?

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 3-Brigitte-0000000625EA.0B2-I hope not.mp3
lines.transcript: I hope not.

lines.filename: 4-Reinhardt-0000000616B7.0B2-Then why bother_ You'll just miss the wind blowing in your hair.mp3
lines.transcript: Then why bother? You'll just miss the wind blowing in your hair.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-0000000625F1.0B2-Bastion, is something wrong_.mp3
lines.transcript: Bastion, is something wrong?

lines.speaker: Bastion
lines.filename: 2-Bastion-000000062BFD.0B2-(concerned and puzzled beep).mp3
lines.transcript: (concerned and puzzled beep)

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 3-Brigitte-0000000625F2.0B2-Your birdfeed was stolen again_ I'll have to have a word with that hamster.mp3
lines.transcript: Your birdfeed was stolen again? I'll have to have a word with that hamster.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-0000000625F5.0B2-Hammond, did you eat Ganymede's birdfeed again_.mp3
lines.transcript: Hammond, did you eat Ganymede's birdfeed again?

lines.speaker: Wrecking Ball
lines.filename: 2-Wrecking Ball-00000006261E.0B2-(hamster noises) He asks whether it's still called birdfeed if a hamster eats it.mp3
lines.transcript: (hamster noises) He asks whether it's still called birdfeed if a hamster eats it.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 3-Brigitte-0000000625F6.0B2-That's a shame. I'll be giving these cookies I made to teammates who deserve them.mp3
lines.transcript: That's a shame. I'll be giving these cookies I made to teammates who deserve them.

lines.filename: 4-Wrecking Ball-00000006261F.0B2-(hamster noises) Translation failure. Profanity database error.mp3
lines.transcript: (hamster noises) Translation failure. Profanity database error.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-0000000625FC.0B2-Real glory comes from helping people, not hurting them.mp3
lines.transcript: Real glory comes from helping people, not hurting them.

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 2-Ashe-0000000616E9.0B2-We may have different definitions.mp3
lines.transcript: We may have different definitions.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-000000062603.0B2-If you don't mind me asking, what is your armor made of_.mp3
lines.transcript: If you don't mind me asking, what is your armor made of?

lines.filename: 2-Echo-0000000614E6.0B2-Your father has my blueprints. Why don't you see for yourself_ I'd hate to spoil the surprise.mp3
lines.transcript: Your father has my blueprints. Why don't you see for yourself? I'd hate to spoil the surprise.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-0000000610DB.0B2-You got your wish, Papa. That's Hyperion alloy.mp3
lines.transcript: You got your wish, Papa. That's Hyperion alloy.

lines.speaker: Reinhardt
lines.filename: 2-Reinhardt-0000000610DD.0B2-Is that a problem_.mp3
lines.transcript: Is that a problem?

lines.speaker: Torbjörn
lines.filename: 3-Torbjörn-0000000610DE.0B2-No. Well, I mean. Maybe.mp3
lines.transcript: No. Well, I mean. Maybe.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-00000006260B.0B2-My dad says a lot of your missions were disasters.mp3
lines.transcript: My dad says a lot of your missions were disasters.

lines.filename: 2-Cassidy-00000006226B.0B2-Well if he’d made me the robo-horse I asked for, maybe I would have stood a better chance.mp3
lines.transcript: Well if he d made me the robo-horse I asked for, maybe I would have stood a better chance.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-000000064459.0B2-Papa, is there anything you'd like to say to our teammates_.mp3
lines.transcript: Papa, is there anything you'd like to say to our teammates?

lines.speaker: Torbjörn
lines.filename: 2-Torbjörn-000000063E7B.0B2-I'm... looking forward to working with you.mp3
lines.transcript: I'm... looking forward to working with you.

lines.speaker: Echo
lines.filename: 3-Echo-00000006380F.0B2-Likewise.mp3
lines.transcript: Likewise.

lines.speaker: Zenyatta
lines.filename: 4-Zenyatta-0000000635E5.0B2-It will be a pleasure.mp3
lines.transcript: It will be a pleasure.

lines.speaker: Orisa

lines.transcript: And I with you.

lines.speaker: Ramattra
lines.filename: 6-Ramattra-000000064005.0B2-How gracious of you.mp3
lines.transcript: How gracious of you.

lines.speaker: Torbjörn
lines.filename: 7-Torbjörn-000000063E7C.0B2-Yes, well. Let's get this done.mp3
lines.transcript: Yes, well. Let's get this done.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-00000006445B.0B2-Papa, don't the Martins live here_.mp3
lines.transcript: Papa, don't the Martins live here?

lines.speaker: Torbjörn
lines.filename: 2-Torbjörn-000000063E7D.0B2-No, they mostly hang out in that Arcology of theirs.mp3
lines.transcript: No, they mostly hang out in that Arcology of theirs.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 3-Brigitte-00000006445C.0B2-Are they still having problems with curious whales_.mp3
lines.transcript: Are they still having problems with curious whales?

lines.speaker: Torbjörn
lines.filename: 4-Torbjörn-000000063E7E.0B2-Yes, and they still won't let me help!.mp3
lines.transcript: Yes, and they still won't let me help!

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-000000065916.0B2-Uncle, I can tell your shoulder is bothering you again.mp3
lines.transcript: Uncle, I can tell your shoulder is bothering you again.

lines.speaker: Reinhardt
lines.filename: 2-Reinhardt-000000065915.0B2-It just sticks a little now and then, like a rusty door. I'm fine.mp3
lines.transcript: It just sticks a little now and then, like a rusty door. I'm fine.

lines.speaker: Sojourn
lines.filename: 3-Sojourn-00000006591A.0B2-Maybe you should run into a few more walls. Get the kinks out.mp3
lines.transcript: Maybe you should run into a few more walls. Get the kinks out.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 4-Brigitte-000000065910.0B2-I keep telling him to stop missing so much.mp3
lines.transcript: I keep telling him to stop missing so much.

lines.speaker: Reinhardt
lines.filename: 5-Reinhardt-000000065914.0B2-That's it. I'm getting off the train.mp3
lines.transcript: That's it. I'm getting off the train.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-0000000658FC.0B2-Didn't Null Sector try all of this once before_ In London_.mp3
lines.transcript: Didn't Null Sector try all of this once before? In London?

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 2-Baptiste-0000000658FD.0B2-I saw an old video about a Null Sector attack in London. Was it like this_.mp3
lines.transcript: I saw an old video about a Null Sector attack in London. Was it like this?

lines.speaker: Lúcio
lines.filename: 3-Lúcio-0000000658FE.0B2-Didn't Null Sector once attack London or something_.mp3
lines.transcript: Didn't Null Sector once attack London or something?

lines.speaker: Sojourn
lines.filename: 4-Sojourn-0000000658F5.0B2-Yeah, except this time, whoever made these robots is invading every nation at once.mp3
lines.transcript: Yeah, except this time, whoever made these robots is invading every nation at once.

lines.speaker: Sojourn
lines.filename: 5-Sojourn-0000000658F6.0B2-Let's focus on helping that transit worker, huh_ We can save the world another day.mp3
lines.transcript: Let's focus on helping that transit worker, huh? We can save the world another day.

lines.filename: 6-Lúcio-00000005BBAE.0B2-All right.mp3
lines.transcript: All right...

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 7-Brigitte-00000005BBAD.0B2-(sigh) I know.mp3
lines.transcript: (sigh) I know.

lines.speaker: Baptiste
lines.filename: 8-Baptiste-00000005BBAC.0B2-_Sigh_ I guess.mp3
lines.transcript: *Sigh* I guess.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-000000067209.0B2-I've been thinking about getting another striped cat, but I'm running out of cool names.mp3
lines.transcript: I've been thinking about getting another striped cat, but I'm running out of cool names...

lines.speaker: Illari
lines.filename: 2-Illari-000000066DB3.0B2-I named my llama _Chuño_. Like the freeze-dried potato.mp3
lines.transcript: I named my llama "Chuño". Like the freeze-dried potato.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 3-Brigitte-000000067208.0B2-Potato... You're a genius! I'll call him Hasselback!.mp3
lines.transcript: Potato... You're a genius! I'll call him Hasselback!

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-00000005AA35.0B2-Energy concentrating in the Titan's chest laser. It's about to fire!.mp3
lines.transcript: Energy concentrating in the Titan's chest laser. It's about to fire!

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-0000000686EE.0B2-_Sky shining orange_... What rhymes with orange_.mp3
lines.transcript: "Sky shining orange"... What rhymes with orange?

lines.speaker: Hazard
lines.filename: 2-Hazard-0000000684E2.0B2-Uh... sky's more of a blue, int it_.mp3
lines.transcript: Uh... sky's more of a blue, int it?

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 3-Brigitte-0000000686EF.0B2-(soft sigh) You aren't a poet, are you_.mp3
lines.transcript: (soft sigh) You aren't a poet, are you?

lines.speaker: Hazard
lines.filename: 4-Hazard-0000000684AD.0B2-If I was, I wouldnae know it.mp3
lines.transcript: If I was, I wouldnae know it.

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 1-Brigitte-00000006B30A.0B2-Lúcio and I have been thinking of throwing a little welcome party for you new recruits! You in_.mp3
lines.transcript: Lúcio and I have been thinking of throwing a little welcome party for you new recruits! You in?

lines.speaker: Anran
lines.filename: 2-Anran-00000006AD9F.0B2-Do we really have time to be relaxing with everything going on_.mp3
lines.transcript: Do we really have time to be relaxing with everything going on?

lines.speaker: Brigitte
lines.filename: 3-Brigitte-00000006B30B.0B2-Sure we do. If we don't keep morale up, we won't be in any shape to fight.mp3
lines.transcript: Sure we do. If we don't keep morale up, we won't be in any shape to fight.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000002ECE1.0B2-What's home like for you, archer_.mp3
lines.transcript: What's home like for you, archer?

lines.speaker: Hanzo
lines.filename: 2-Hanzo-00000002F2C6.0B2-A village high atop a hill. There are cherry blossoms in the spring. I miss it dearly.mp3
lines.transcript: A village high atop a hill. There are cherry blossoms in the spring. I miss it dearly.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000002ECE2.0B2-I can tell you and the soldier have some history together.mp3
lines.transcript: I can tell you and the soldier have some history together.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 2-Ana-00000002EDF7.0B2-This is not our first battle together. And not the last… unless he does something foolish.mp3
lines.transcript: This is not our first battle together. And not the last&  unless he does something foolish.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000002ECDD.0B2-You seem awful familiar. You sure we haven't met before_.mp3
lines.transcript: You seem awful familiar. You sure we haven't met before?

lines.speaker: Soldier_ 76
lines.filename: 2-Soldier_ 76-00000002EEE3.0B2-Maybe in another life.mp3
lines.transcript: Maybe in another life.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000002ECE7.0B2-You ask me, you pump something full of these chemicals, electricity, and whatnot… That's not a man, it'.mp3
lines.transcript: You ask me, you pump something full of these chemicals, electricity, and whatnot&  That's not a man, it's a monster.

lines.speaker: Soldier_ 76
lines.filename: 2-Soldier_ 76-00000002EF28.0B2-Maybe so… Or maybe they're not as different as you'd think.mp3
lines.transcript: Maybe so&  Or maybe they're not as different as you'd think.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000002ECDE.0B2-You don't seem like someone who'd put his faith in alchemy.mp3
lines.transcript: You don't seem like someone who'd put his faith in alchemy.

lines.speaker: Soldier_ 76
lines.filename: 2-Soldier_ 76-00000002EF40.0B2-You have to believe in something. May as well be the thing that keeps you alive.mp3
lines.transcript: You have to believe in something. May as well be the thing that keeps you alive.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000002ECDB.0B2-So, where you from, back home_.mp3
lines.transcript: So, where you from, back home?

lines.speaker: Soldier_ 76
lines.filename: 2-Soldier_ 76-00000002EEFB.0B2-I don't stay in one place for too long. Came from a town called Bloomington. Indiana.mp3
lines.transcript: I don't stay in one place for too long. Came from a town called Bloomington. Indiana.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000002ECDC.0B2-Didn't expect to find another American so far from home. You fight in the war_.mp3
lines.transcript: Didn't expect to find another American so far from home. You fight in the war?

lines.speaker: Soldier_ 76
lines.filename: 2-Soldier_ 76-00000002EF26.0B2-There's a man like me fighting in every war.mp3
lines.transcript: There's a man like me fighting in every war.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000002ECE3.0B2-Not sure how I feel about all this alchemy nonsense. Seems like hocus pocus to me.mp3
lines.transcript: Not sure how I feel about all this alchemy nonsense. Seems like hocus pocus to me.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 2-Ana-00000002EDED.0B2-I could leave you to die, if you prefer.mp3
lines.transcript: I could leave you to die, if you prefer.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000002ECE0.0B2-You're not bad with a bow, but don't you think it's a little old fashioned_.mp3
lines.transcript: You're not bad with a bow, but don't you think it's a little old fashioned?

lines.speaker: Hanzo
lines.filename: 2-Hanzo-00000002F2C5.0B2-Perhaps there is still a future for anachronisms.mp3
lines.transcript: Perhaps there is still a future for anachronisms.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000002ECDF.0B2-You think this lord's good for the money_ Castle looks like it's seen better days.mp3
lines.transcript: You think this lord's good for the money? Castle looks like it's seen better days.

lines.speaker: Hanzo
lines.filename: 2-Hanzo-00000002F2CF.0B2-If he breaks his word, he has made some powerful enemies.mp3
lines.transcript: If he breaks his word, he has made some powerful enemies.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000002ECE8.0B2-Hey, archer, what say you pass that flask around so we can all have a drink_ This is thirsty work.mp3
lines.transcript: Hey, archer, what say you pass that flask around so we can all have a drink? This is thirsty work.

lines.speaker: Hanzo
lines.filename: 2-Hanzo-00000002F2C8.0B2-We drink when the job is done.mp3
lines.transcript: We drink when the job is done.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000002ECE9.0B2-You ever heard of Robin Hood_.mp3
lines.transcript: You ever heard of Robin Hood?

lines.speaker: Hanzo
lines.filename: 2-Hanzo-00000002F2C9.0B2-Indeed. We have our own version_ Ishikawa Goemon. He was boiled alive.mp3
lines.transcript: Indeed. We have our own version: Ishikawa Goemon. He was boiled alive.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000004A530.0B2-What's it like Genji_ You know... as a cyborg.mp3
lines.transcript: What's it like Genji? You know... as a cyborg.

lines.speaker: Genji
lines.filename: 2-Genji-00000004BD04.0B2-Pain and agony. And what am I_ Man or machine_.mp3
lines.transcript: Pain and agony. And what am I? Man or machine?

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 3-Cassidy-00000004A52F.0B2-Then why did you agree to it_.mp3
lines.transcript: Then why did you agree to it?

lines.speaker: Genji
lines.filename: 4-Genji-00000004BD03.0B2-I wanted to be able to walk again.mp3
lines.transcript: I wanted to be able to walk again.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000004C406.0B2-Anyone else hungry_.mp3
lines.transcript: Anyone else hungry?

lines.speaker: Genji
lines.transcript: No.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000004C3F7.0B2-You serious_.mp3
lines.transcript: You serious?

lines.transcript: You weren t given those guns to toss them around like trash.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000002A87F.0B2-I heard there's a nice reward for bringing you fellas in.mp3

lines.speaker: Junkrat

lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-000000052B9E.0B2-I heard there's a nice reward for bringing you fellas in.mp3

lines.transcript: It was a long and difficult road... but now I feel at ease in my own body.

lines.transcript: How'd a guy like you get a nickname like "Sparrow" anyways?

lines.filename: 2-Genji-00000002A7C1.0B2-My father, Sojiro, called me that as an insult. I've grown to like it.mp3

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000002ECA3.0B2-Just remember, Pharah, we ain't playing with toy guns anymore.mp3
lines.transcript: Just remember, Pharah, we ain't playing with toy guns anymore.

lines.transcript: Too many suitors.

lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-000000054FD8.0B2-I heard your tale's a tragic one.mp3
lines.transcript: I heard your tale's a tragic one.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 3-Cassidy-000000054FBC.0B2-The math's a bit concerning on that.mp3

lines.filename: 3-Cassidy-000000054FB2.0B2-It was a footrest.mp3

lines.transcript: Too rich.

lines.transcript: Why is every scrap we get into a last stand?

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000005E9D9.0B2-You remind me of myself when I was younger.mp3

lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-000000062256.0B2-This one might get tricky. Can I count on you to watch my back_.mp3

lines.filename: 3-Cassidy-000000062254.0B2-You've told me about your family. Not sure that's a compliment.mp3

lines.transcript: Think I owe you a round, ma'am. Those names you gave me have turned out to be mighty fine recruits.

lines.filename: 3-Cassidy-000000062259.0B2-You’re supposed to stop them first.mp3

lines.filename: 4-Junkrat-00000006098D.0B2-I do stop them. I think my problem is that I stop them a little too hard.mp3
lines.transcript: I do stop them. I think my problem is that I stop them a little too hard.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000006225D.0B2-You know, I haven't had any real competition at the range lately.mp3

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.filename: 2-Ana-0000000640FD.0B2-There are plenty of moving targets on the battlefield.mp3
lines.transcript: There are plenty of moving targets on the battlefield.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 3-Cassidy-00000006225E.0B2-I'll take that as a no.mp3
lines.transcript: I'll take that as a no.

lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-000000062260.0B2-I'd get dizzy always rolling around like that. Tell me you're always right side up.mp3
lines.transcript: I'd get dizzy always rolling around like that. Tell me you're always right side up.

lines.filename: 2-Wrecking Ball-000000062622.0B2-(hamster noises) The hamster says that unlike you, he does not imitate tumbleweeds.mp3
lines.transcript: (hamster noises) The hamster says that unlike you, he does not imitate tumbleweeds.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 3-Cassidy-00000006225F.0B2-Hey now, what's that supposed to mean_.mp3

lines.filename: 2-Bastion-000000062BFB.0B2-(regretful beeps).mp3
lines.transcript: (regretful beeps)

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 3-Cassidy-00000006226A.0B2-Of course not. Would be wholly unlike Lindholm to give you something useful.mp3

lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-000000062272.0B2-Hey Dr. Zhou. Is it gonna rain_.mp3

lines.transcript: Looks like a 30% chance of precipitation.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 3-Cassidy-000000062273.0B2-So... maybe. (sigh) The marvels of modern science.mp3
lines.transcript: So... maybe. (sigh) The marvels of modern science.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-000000062278.0B2-I’ll never forgive us for what we did in Hanamura.mp3
lines.transcript: I ll never forgive us for what we did in Hanamura.

lines.filename: 2-Soldier_ 76-000000062F5E.0B2-Those people were criminals.mp3
lines.transcript: Those people were criminals.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 3-Cassidy-000000062279.0B2-So were we.mp3
lines.transcript: So were we.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-0000000642BE.0B2-You always bring out the best in me.mp3
lines.transcript: You always bring out the best in me.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.transcript: Oh, enough.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-0000000642CB.0B2-A friend told me that you're getting rusty.mp3
lines.transcript: A friend told me that you're getting rusty.

lines.speaker: Reaper
lines.filename: 2-Reaper-00000006384F.0B2-You don't have friends.mp3
lines.transcript: You don't have friends.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000006A1D5.0B2-I hear a lot of folks say _chivalry is dead_, but I'm glad to see it's alive and well with you, partner.mp3
lines.transcript: I hear a lot of folks say "chivalry is dead", but I'm glad to see it's alive and well with you, partner.

lines.speaker: Reinhardt
lines.filename: 2-Reinhardt-00000006B1FD.0B2-And look at you, making the old west young again!.mp3
lines.transcript: And look at you, making the old west young again!

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 3-Cassidy-00000006A1C9.0B2-(chuckle) Oh, I think I've seen too many sunsets to call myself young.mp3
lines.transcript: (chuckle) Oh, I think I've seen too many sunsets to call myself young.

lines.filename: 4-Reinhardt-00000006B1FE.0B2-Young or old, I'll be proud to chase the next one by your side!.mp3
lines.transcript: Young or old, I'll be proud to chase the next one by your side!

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-000000066837.0B2-You might be able to beat me in basketball, but how about a couple rounds of bowling_.mp3

lines.filename: 2-Pharah-00000006576F.0B2-So you want me to beat you at every sport then_.mp3

lines.filename: 3-Mercy-000000065F31.0B2-Ooh! If I come, can we play with bumpers_.mp3

lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000006A1CA.0B2-D.Va, you said you wanted to tell me more about this Pachi fella's friends_.mp3

lines.filename: 2-D.Va-000000069B96.0B2-Oh yeah, so there's Geranman, he's pretty new to the crew.mp3

lines.filename: 3-Cassidy-00000006A1CB.0B2-Right. He's got that square around his mouth.mp3

lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000006A7EE.0B2-Can't you do something about the glare when you get all up in the sky like that_.mp3

lines.filename: 2-Illari-00000006A5AB.0B2-Turn down the brightness on your flashbang. Then we'll talk.mp3
lines.transcript: Turn down the brightness on your flashbang. Then we'll talk.

lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000006A1C8.0B2-I'm feeling generous today, Ashe. Think I might just remember where I left your bike, after all.mp3

lines.speaker: Ashe
lines.filename: 2-Ashe-0000000688CD.0B2-I don't want it.mp3
lines.transcript: I don't want it.

lines.transcript: Someone has to keep you in order, Cole.

lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000006A1D6.0B2-You ain’t the only one who tried bounty hunting when Overwatch fell through.mp3

lines.transcript: Hm. You gave up quick.

lines.speaker: Cassidy
lines.filename: 3-Cassidy-00000006A1D7.0B2-Well, I realized that huntin' ain't always helpin'. Reckon you might feel the same, someday.mp3
lines.transcript: Well, I realized that huntin' ain't always helpin'. Reckon you might feel the same, someday.

lines.transcript: No one wanted my help. At least this way, I'm useful.

lines.transcript: Your father was a bad man, but he wasn't a tyrant. This ain't what he would've wanted.

lines.transcript: If you think my ambitions end at my father's wants, you're more fool than you look.

lines.filename: 1-Cassidy-00000006A7EA.0B2-Was a little jealous of you back in the day, you know. Always made everything look so easy.mp3

lines.speaker: Cassidy

lines.transcript: Cassidy, you're going to teach her to beg! ... Aww, but she has been so good lately, hasn't she?

lines.filename: 1-Claire-00000005E5A1.0B2-I saw you flying around up there. It looked exhausting.mp3
